campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including money the city has invested and how much cash is available. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, including walking, biking, and public transit options.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city financial accounts
Treasurer's monthly cash and fund balance report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan development (walking, biking, transit improvements)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the usual first formal reading and planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
Ordinance (new city rule) introduced
First reading waived to speed up approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve records from several previous meetings held in March. The council also authorized the town manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details were cut off in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes from multiple March 2026 sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (project details unclear from agenda)
Town manager given authority to execute construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and prepare their upcoming agendas for April 21 and April 28, 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, decided which items needed specific time slots for discussion, and checked that all required notices were properly filed.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 meeting agenda
Reviewed and organized the April 28, 2026 draft agenda
Assigned time slots to specific agenda items for efficient meeting flow
Verified public notice requirements were met for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and infrastructure projects will get funding. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for city construction and improvement projects
Teen Month proclamation and recognition
National Volunteer Week proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting minutes from March and discuss changes to tenant protections for renters in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update rules in Chapter 19.71 of the city code governing how residential tenants are protected.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 commission meeting minutes
New ordinance amending tenant protection rules for residential renters
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and community training
City budget information now available to the public
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
How to track city spending and legislative influence

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 15, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's charter (its basic governing rules). Chair Pat Nikolai joined the meeting remotely from Oregon, while the city offered both in-person and online options for residents to attend and share their thoughts.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings being held
Remote participation options via Zoom for residents
Chair Pat Nikolai attending from Oregon
Public comment submission deadline via email to city attorney

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative and planning items. The main focus was reviewing a major update to the city's 5-year housing and community development plan, reviewing requests from council members for new projects to work on, and considering changes to how the city fills council vacancies.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 community development and housing plan
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
New project requests from individual council members
Approval of meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize the building as more historically significant, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of new space as part of seismic safety improvements. The Council also approved meeting notes from the Historic Resources Board's previous gathering.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building getting upgraded historic status
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone project
Historic Resources Board schedule and assignments
